violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (the grip on the slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

Quinoa, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The floor in the walk in freezer, and in the paper dry storage area have an accumulation of soil residue and debris. The ceiling tiles in the dishwashing area have an accumulation of food residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils, such as mandolins and wedger, were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
Utensils with food contact surfaces observed in draw with unclean assorted items not used for food. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
The ice machine condenser has an accumulation of dust residue and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
The sharp edges on the splash guard at kitchen hand washing sink is covered with duct tape in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
The fan covers in the walk-in (letter G) is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024

Quinoa, tofu, and chicken observed held at 46-55 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were placed in refrigerator to rapidly chill. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. cauliflower was placed hot in the refrigerator in wrapped plastic container greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

Salads packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen and service line area. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
Cutting boards on the pizza side serving line observed not in good repair, with the edges flaking off. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: rolling racks can opener base service line shelves carts bread dolly New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

The counter in the pizza side area is in poor repair, cracked with a gap, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2024

The floor in the dry storage area has an accumulation of soil residue, trash, and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/01/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed on the stairway and in the grease recycling area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2023
Ceiling tiles in the dry storage area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/01/2024
